signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Founded in 1976, Efes won its first Turkish League trophy in 1979 but did not become a true powerhouse until the 1990s, when Coach Aydin Ors led the team to five Turkish championships in six seasons plus the 1993 Saporta Cup Final. Efes became the first Turkish club to win a European title when it captured the 1996 Korac Cup. Efes was also the first Turkish team to reach the EuroLeague Final Four in 2000, a milestone it repeated in the 2001 SuproLeague. The club next tasted glory when Coach Ergin Ataman returned to Efes and put together a team for the ages, led by Shane Larkin, Vasilije Micic and Bryant Dunston. Efes reached the EuroLeague Championship Game in 2019 and led the 2019-20 EuroLeague when it was canceled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. A year later, the club’s ascent to the summit was successfully concluded with a championship game victory over FC Barcelona in Cologne with Micic named both EuroLeague and Final Four MVP. Efes successfully defended the title in 2022, defeating Real in the championship game behind Final Four MVP Micic. Efes missed the playoffs in 2023 and lost in the Play-In Showdown in 2024. Last season Efes bowed out after a thrilling five-game series against Panathinaikos Athens and its former Coach Ataman. Now, with Coach Igor Kokoskov in charge, Efes continues its mission to battle for trophies in every competition it enters.

heroMedia.title: Swider’s buzzer-beater seals Efes’s dramatic comeback in Munich
heroMedia.summary: The Turkish side’s second-half charge was sparked by Jordan Loyd

linkedIds.text: FC Bayern Munich v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-03-12T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
Anadolu Efes Istanbul pulled off a thrilling 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich, capped by a buzzer-beater from Cole Swider that ended the team’s long road losing streak dating back to December and moved its record to 10-21.

Swider had been on the floor for just 3 minutes, but when the coach called his number, he delivered. “Pablo called on me and I try to keep this fireman’s mentality whenever I'm called on, whenever there's a fire, I try and put it out in any sense,’’ said Swider.

‘‘The guys did such a great job coming back in the second half, they deserve the interview, but, yeah, it felt great to win with the team.’’

Efes struggled to find its rhythm in the first half, with Bayern controlling the pace, building a 13-point lead, and exploiting defensive gaps. The visitors got scoring from just six players and had trouble organizing offensively and moving the ball, committing 9 turnovers in the first half.

However, the second half told a different story for Efes. Led by Jordan Loyd, Efes found a better offensive rhythm and gradually clawed its way back to close the gap. After scoring just 26 points in the first half, the visitors racked up 55 in the second, turning the momentum in their favor.

After a quiet first half with 5 points, Jordan Loyd erupted for 15 points in the last 20 minutes, driving Efes’s second-half comeback.

‘‘This group of guys, we’ve been through so much this year,’’ said Loyd after the game. ‘‘And to be able to play some good basketball in the second half and put together a win on the road is huge for us. We’re trying to be as positive as can be, no matter the result, and I’m just super happy for the club.’’

After hitting just 3-of-13 from deep in the first half, Efes found its range in the second, knocking down 9 three-pointers and finishing 44.4% from deep. PJ Dozier stepped up, scoring 12 points after a scoreless first half, while Sehmus Hazer added 11 to keep Efes in the game.

‘‘I'm very proud of them, as a coach,’’ said Pablo Laso after the game. ‘‘I think our first half was not good. We were even playing worse because we were not making shots, and that was coming down in our energy. I think in the second half we were able to make the same shots, we moved the ball well, we got good looks, good shots, and the game-winners.’’

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Anadolu Efes Spor Kulübü (@anadoluefessk)

Bayern, which had controlled the momentum in the first half, struggled to find its shooting rhythm after the interval and committed turnovers as Efes tightened its defense. Xavier Rathan-Mayes led Bayern with 15 points, while Andreas Obst had a quiet night from deep, hitting just 1-of-7 three pointers to finish with 13 points.

‘‘Two different games tonight. First half, excellent game for us. And second, offense, defense, not on the level how we wanted to play, and we didn't find solutions in offense, especially against very aggressive defense from Efes. We didn't react very good after the execution, and they used this,’’ said Coach Svetislav Pesic after the game.

linkedId.text: FC Bayern Munich v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-03-12T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Swider’s buzzer-beater seals Efes’s dramatic comeback in Munich
articleMetadata.description: Anadolu Efes Istanbul pulled off a thrilling 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich, capped by a buzzer-beater from Cole Swider that ended the team’s long road losing streak dating back to December and moved its record to 10-21.

heroMedia.title: ASVEL 'has fun' and keeps Efes's number, wins 88-91 in Istanbul
heroMedia.summary: ASVEL has five straight wins against Efes

linkedIds.text: Anadolu Efes Istanbul v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-06T17:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/asv/) lost its first 11 road games this season but has now won two of its last four after holding off [Anadolu Efes Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/anadolu-efes-istanbul/ist/) 88-91 on Friday night. This also marked ASVEL's fifth consecutive win against Efes, though it still couldn't lift the team from last place in the standings, where it sits with an 8-22 record.

"We don't win too many games, so I have to be satisfied. I can't be anything but satisfied after a game like this," ASVEL head coach Pierric Poupet said in a postgame EuroLeague TV interview.

content.title: Anadolu Efes Istanbul-LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
In a turnover-filled game with 35 combined miscues, ASVEL managed to survive a late charge by Efes led by [Sehmus Hazer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sehmus-hazer/011201/), who scored 8 of his career-high 20 points in the final 2:20. But ASVEL had [Braian Angola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/braian-angola/010465/), who finished with 26 points – a personal best in his seventh EuroLeague game.

"We came with an idea to play as a team, play together," Angola said in a postgame EuroLeague TV interview. "I think my teammates have a lot of trust in me and my quality, and my coach has the same. He always tells me to go out there and have fun. When you have that type of confidence from your coach and your teammates, it is always great to play basketball and have fun."

ASVEL opened up a 12-point lead in the first quarter behind [Zac Seljaas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/zachary-seljaas/014088/) and [Armel Traore](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/armel-traore/010319/), but [Saben Lee](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/saben-lee/013389/) entered late in the first quarter and the deficit started melting. Three-pointers from Seljaas and Angola kept ASVEL ahead, but [Rolands Smits](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/rolands-smits/007109/) hit a pair of triples, and Lee continued to harass ASVEL's defense as the hosts went in front 42-40. However, in the final two minutes of the half, Heurtel hit a pair of three-pointers, including one with the final second, to make it 47-49 at halftime.

Les paniers et le sang froid qu’il faut sur la fin.

MVP Braian ⭐️ pic.twitter.com/YUw85u78zJ

— LDLC ASVEL (@LDLCASVEL) March 6, 2026

Apart from the opening minute, ASVEL remained ahead the entire second half, but had to navigate considerable drama. The third quarter saw the two teams combine for 12 turnovers. ASVEL used that to stay ahead and pull away further in the fourth quarter with a three from Angola to make it 65-73, then as Efes threatened to rally, back-to-back threes from [Glynn Watson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/glynn-watson/014013/) extended the lead to 76-83.

But Smits hit from long range once, then Hazer connected twice to get the hosts within 86-87. ASVEL didn't score a field goal in the remaining time, but [David Lighty](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-lighty/003526/) made a defensive play forcing Lee's offensive foul, and Angola was perfect from the foul line to preserve ASVEL's win.

"The plan is to keep fighting until the end, not just today, but all the way through the season. To see if we can leave somebody behind us," Poupet said.

The fight was also evident in ASVEL's 11-5 advantage in offensive rebounds. Though ASVEL closed in on Efes, the team will need more wins to escape the bottom of the standings. This victory, however, could prove important in that regard, as ASVEL now holds a season sweep of Efes, which dropped to 9-21.

linkedId.text: Anadolu Efes Istanbul v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-06T17:30:00.000Z)

heroMedia.title: Efes's Shane Larkin out 4-6 more weeks
heroMedia.summary: The two-time EuroLeague champion is sidelined due to a groin injury

content.text:
Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard Shane Larkin is expected to stay sidelined for at least four more weeks due to a groin injury, the club confirmed Thursday. Larkin went through tests that discarded surgery, but confirmed that his treatment is not completed. Larkin's last EuroLeague game was against FC Bayern Munich on November 14, 2025. He is expected to be our for 4-6 weeks and may miss the remainder of the EuroLeague Regular Season. He averaged 15.8 points, 4.5 assists and a PIR of 15.6 so far this season. 

"As you can imagine, this is a difficult situation for our team. We want our captain, Shane Larkin, to be fully healthy and hope he will rejoin us as soon as possible. I would like to thank our club's medical team, Shane Larkin, and our club organization for their dedicated work during this process," Efes head coach Pablo Laso said. "We tried to get our captain to play in the Turkish Cup, but it wasn't possible. When I joined the team, I knew Shane Larkin would be out for a while. Now I hope he will be back with us as soon as possible."

Kasık bölgesinden sakatlığı bulunan Kaptanımız Shane Larkin'in planlı kontrol muayenesi Prof. Dr. Barış Kocaoğlu tarafından gerçekleştirilmiş, yapılan kapsamlı tetkiklerde oyuncumuz için yeni bir cerrahi muayeneye gerek bulunmadığı fakat Kaptanımızın tedavi sürecinin henüz… pic.twitter.com/T8aEt8A7Ma

— Anadolu Efes SK (@AnadoluEfesSK) March 5, 2026

The two-time EuroLeague champion is sidelined due to a groin injury

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Jared Butler’s blitz and inside dominance fuels Zvezda's victory over Efes
heroMedia.summary: The Serbian team kept on pushing for a top-six finish with a hard-earned win

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-02-25T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/) showed two distinct offensive faces to hold off [Anadolu Efes Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/anadolu-efes-istanbul/ist/) and grab a key 91-81 home victory on Wednesday night.

The first aspect of the Serbian team’s play was shooting from beyond the arc, led by [Jared Butler](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-butler/014163/) firing 7-of-9 from three-point range. He was supported by [Nikola Kalinic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-kalinic/004866/) with 2 of his 3 attempts, while [Codi Miller-McIntyre](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/codi-miller-mcintyre/009048/) also made a pair and [Ognjen Dobric](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/ognjen-dobric/004007/) stringed his only long-range shot, allowing the team to make a more than creditable 12-of-24 three-pointers (50%).

However, that Butler-inspired barrage from deep was never enough to establish a game-clinching lead against an Efes team that played with balance and discipline to stay in contention nearly until the final buzzer. Although Zvezda led all the way after moving ahead, 35-32, with a Kalinic triple 3 minutes before halftime, the margin only reached a level of comfort late in the fourth quarter – and that was nothing to do with long-range shooting.

content.title: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade-Anadolu Efes Istanbul | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Instead, Sasa Obradovic’s team gradually pulled into a dominant lead through a final period of inside dominance at both ends of the floor. Between Kalinic making his second triple with 2:49 to play in the third quarter, and Butler draining his seventh with just over a minute remaining, the home team did not make a single three-point strike.

During that phase of nearly 12 minutes, Zvezda turned its attention to aggressive drives to the basket, scoring 7 two-pointers with [Chima Moneke](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/chima-moneke/012201/) and [Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) both netting three-point plays. At the same time, Zvezda also flexed its defensive muscles, holding Efes to only one field goal conversion during the middle 6 minutes of the fourth quarter.

Although Efes managed to compete physically for much of the game, Zvezda’s gradually increasing physical dominance allowed the home team to come away with a 41-32 rebounding edge, including 13 offensive rebounds, which resulted in 12 second-chance points.

МВП, статистика и најистакнутији појединци утакмице 29. кола @EuroLeague 📊🔴⚪️#kkcz #WeAreTheTeam pic.twitter.com/bsNtlxJGEy

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) February 25, 2026

The victory was orchestrated by Miller-McIntyre, whose overall contribution was apparent from his combination of 12 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists with just 1 turnover. And the point guard was clear about the key to his team’s triumph, pinpointing Zvezda’s success in turning back an Efes third-quarter burst of 25 points in just over 8 minutes.

“Efes is a good team with great players and they’re going to make a run, which they did,” explained Miller-McIntyre. “But we were able to stay calm in those moments and come out with the win. If we can get stops and run, we’re phenomenal. But we need to know when to slow it down, when to push the pace, and I think we did a great job with that tonight.

“When we focus on defense, I think we can beat anybody, or at least be in the game with anybody, and when they made that run, we found a way to get stops and convert on the offensive side.”

It was an important win for Zvezda, which snapped a two-game losing streak in the EuroLeague and came immediately after the emotional high of a Serbian Cup triumph last weekend.

❤️🤍 https://t.co/EFRmjqdg52

— KK Crvena zvezda Meridianbet (@kkcrvenazvezda) February 25, 2026

Coach Obradovic admitted his players were fatigued after the efforts they exerted to take that trophy, but he hailed their ability to close out the game in the crucial stages, telling EuroLeague TV: “We missed physicality, but in some important moments we found a way, especially with the defense. The effort was over the line in this moment.

“In the last quarter, we did great things, and again it shows the character of the team.”

Efes coach Pablo Laso, meanwhile, insisted his team will not give up on the season despite being seven wins away from the play-in places.

“We are representing a club that always fights, and needs to fight, until the end in every game,” he said in the postgame press conference.

“We have to continue playing the best way possible. The play-in looks very far away, for sure, but I don’t understand another way to be a better basketball team than this. So we have to keep fighting, game by game.”

linkedId.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-02-25T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Butler’s blitz and inside dominance fuels Zvezda's victory over Efes
articleMetadata.description: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade showed two distinct offensive faces to hold off Anadolu Efes Istanbul and grab a key 91-81 home victory on Wednesday night, led by Jared Butler firing 7-of-9 from three-point range.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Turkish Cup: Besiktas, Fenerbahce meet in the final
heroMedia.summary: Besiktas beat Efes to return to the championship game. Fenerbahce defeated Turk Telekom

content.text:
[Besiktas GAIN Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/teams/besiktas-gain-istanbul/bes/) and [Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fenerbahce-beko-istanbul/roster/ulk/) are facing each other in the Turkish Cup final for the second consecutive season. 

 

### Horton-Tucker leads Fenerbahce past Turk Telekom

Fenerbahce Beko Istanbl got closer to defend the Turkish Cup title by edging Turk Telekom Ankara 86-78 at Sinan Erdem Sports Hall. Fenerbahce is set to face Besiktas GAIN Istanbul in a rematch of last season's final, and has the chance to win its third consecutive title. 

[Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/) paced the winners with 29 points on 6 of 9 two-pointers and 3 of 3 three-pointers. [Wade Baldwin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/wade-baldwin-iv/009863/) added 17 points while [Mikael Jantunen](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mikael-jantunen/012613/) had 11 for Fenerbahce. [Jaleen Smith](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/jaleen-smith/011192/) led Turk Telekom with 12 points. [Kyle Allman](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/kyle-allman/011924/) and [Marko Simonovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/marko-simonovic/012711/) each added 11 while [Uros Trifunovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/uros-trifunovic/007673/) had 10 for Coach Erden Can's squad. 

Trifunovic and Allman stepped up early before Jordan Usher's three-pointer gave Turk Telekom an 8-15 lead. Usher scored again and Smith buried one from downtown for an 11-20 Turk Telekom lead at the end of the first quarter. Horton-Tucker and [Tarik Biberovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tarik-biberovic/006961/) rescued Fenerbahce and Baldwin's dunk tied it at 26-26. Simonovic followed a dunk with a three-pointer, getting help from Allman and [Kris Bankston](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/kris-bankston/012760/) to restore a 34-42 Turk Telekom lead at halftime. 

Baldwin struck from beyond the arc, igniting a 14-0 run in which Horton-Tucker had six points and which Biberovic capped with a triple, good for a 54-47 score. Smith and Trifunovic got Turk Telekom closer, 60-59, early in the fourth quarter. Jantunen, Baldwin and Horton-Tucker combined for all points in an 8-0 run that boosted Fenerbahce's lead to 68-59. Horton-Tucker struck three times from downtown to reject all Turk Telekom attempts to return to the game. Turk Telekom never gave up but Horton-Tucker and [Nando De Colo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nando-de-colo/002100/) sealed the win from the foul line.

Maç Bizim! Finaldeyiz! 💪

Tebrikler Fenerbahçe Beko! 👏

Maç Sonucu | Fenerbahçe Beko 🆚
Türk Telekom: 86-78

Skor dağılımımız: Horton-Tucker 29, Baldwin 17, Jantunen 11, Onuralp 8, Biberovic 8, De Colo 6, Metecan 3, Boston Jr. 2, Birch 2. #YellowLegacy… pic.twitter.com/uXBoDjPkrX

— Fenerbahçe Beko (@FBBasketbol) February 20, 2026

### Besiktas storms into Turkish Cup final

Besiktas GAIN Istanbul reached the Turkish Cup final by downing Anadolu Efes Istanbul 91-82 in the first semifinal. Besiktas made it to its second consecutive final, with a chance to lift the Turkish Cup trophy for the first time since 2012. [Sertac Sanli](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/sertac-sanli/002968/) led the winners with 18 points. [Jonah Mathews](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/jonah-mathews/011936/) added 12, [Devon Dotson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/devon-dotson/013285/) had 11 while [Conor Morgan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/conor-morgan/009680/) and [Ismael Kamagate](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/ismael-kamagate/011926/) each got 10 for Besiktas. [PJ Dozier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/pj-dozier/012742/) led Efes with 24 points. [Ercan Osmani](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/ercan-osmani/012710/) added 14, [Jordan Loyd](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jordan-loyd/009754/) had 13 while [Cole Swider](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/cole-swider/014025/) got 10 for Efes. 

Morgan struck twice from downtown and Dotson added another one for an early 9-4 Besiktas lead. [Nick Weiler-Babb](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nick-weiler-babb/010391/) and Dozier stepped up for Efes before Osmani's three-point play gave his team a 15-18 lead. [Vitto Brown](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/vitto-brown/013365/) and [Anthony Brown](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/anthony-brown/009025/) each joined the three-point shootout to restore a 23-20 Besiktas edge after 10 minutes. [Matt Thomas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/matt-thomas/008967/) and an unstoppable Sanli boosted their team's lead to 37-31. Sanli got help from [Yigit Arslan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/yigit-arslan/007914/) and Kamagate to make it a double-digit game, 53-39, on a buzzer-beating backdoor layup by Mathews. 

Dozier followed a triple with a baseline jumper to give Efes hope, 58-53, soon after the break. Dozier kept pacing Efes and Osmani's fast-break basket completed his team's comeback, 62-63. Sanli buried a buzzer-beater three-pointer to make Besiktas regain a 71-69 lead at the end of the third quarter. [Vincent Poirier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/vincent-poirier/004887/) helped Efes get a 75-78 lead before Brown followed a three-pointer with a dunk-and-foul for an 81-78 Besiktas edge with under 4 minutes left. Mathews downed an eight-meter triple and Sanli also struck from beyond the arc to seal the outcome.

Nefes kesen maçta galibiyet bizim! Ziraat Bankası Türkiye Kupası'nda finale yükseliyoruz. 💪

Beşiktaş GAİN 91-82 Anadolu Efes | Maç Sonucu#TheBlackEagleRises 🦅 pic.twitter.com/9T40HQw89G

— Beşiktaş GAİN (@BJK_Basketbol) February 20, 2026

⏩ Ziraat Bankası Türkiye Kupası'nda yarı finaldeki rakibimiz Anadolu Efes oldu.

20 Şubat Cuma günü Sinan Erdem Spor Salonu'nda oynanacak karşılaşma, saat 17.15'te başlayacak. pic.twitter.com/BWlExBddiy

— Beşiktaş GAİN (@BJK_Basketbol) February 18, 2026

heroMedia.title: High-flyers: EuroLeague experts pick their favorite dunkers
heroMedia.summary: A panel of EuroLeague voices spotlights the players turning gravity into a guideline

content.text:
The EuroLeague has always been a showcase for skill, execution and strategy, but nothing ignites arenas or social feeds quite like a thunderous dunk. This season, the league’s most explosive high‑flyers are turning routine possessions into must‑see moments, blending power, flair and athleticism that transcend box scores. 

From [Tyrique Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyrique-jones/011981/)’s arena‑shaking slams and [Kai Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kai-jones/014102/)’s sky‑walking artistry to [Kenneth Faried](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kenneth-faried/011284/)’s ageless rim‑rocking energy and [Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/)'s bulldozing slams, the EuroLeague’s best dunkers aren’t just finishing plays – they’re redefining how excitement looks above the rim. As a panel of EuroLeague experts weigh in on their favorite high‑fliers, one thing is clear: the dunk is alive, elevated, and louder than ever.

### Tyrique Jones

*Geoff Gillingham*

Is there anyone in the EuroLeague who makes a crowd erupt like Tyrique Jones? Whether in Partizan’s black and white or Olympiacos red, his rim-rattling dunks – especially at home – send shockwaves through the arena. And it’s not just for show: his presence in the paint forces defenses to collapse, freeing up shooters on the perimeter. Just a few games into his Olympiacos stint, he’s already delivered must-see moments at SEF, while Partizan fans still remember the backboard-bending slams in Belgrade. Whether you’re a new fan or a seasoned diehard, when Tyrique takes off, you can’t help but rise with him. That’s why he’s the league’s most electrifying dunker.

content.title: Tyrique Jones dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Tyrique Jones so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Kai Jones

*Frankie Sachs*

There is no more prolific dunker in the league than Kai Jones, who has 42 dunks among his 45 baskets this season for Anadolu Efes Istanbul. And Jones is no one-trick pony; his dunks include an assortment of put-back jams, alley-oop flushes, power finishes in traffic and showstoppers in the break. Every time Jones touches the ball on offense, he is a threat to stuff it in the hole. The native of the Bahamas competed in the long jump before taking up basketball at age and the athleticism he developed on the track not only serves him well, but is a blessing to every fan who gets a chance to see Jones fly. His dunking abilities are a rare treat and something to be cherished by EuroLeague fans.

content.title: Kai Jones's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Kai Jones so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Talen Horton-Tucker

*Igor Petrinovic*

While there are certainly players with more bounce than Talen Horton-Tucker, he’s currently my favorite dunker in the EuroLeague. With THT, it always feels like an explosive play is about to happen at any moment, and he usually creates that excitement all on his own, often out of nowhere. It’s his style that makes it so special to me. There’s something uniquely thrilling about the way he bulldozes through defenses and powers his way to a high-flying slam. Horton-Tucker’s repertoire includes a variety of dunks, and whether it’s a Statue of Liberty finish or a two-handed, head-at-the-rim power slam, THT adds a facial expression that makes you want to watch the replay over and over again.

content.title: Talen Horton-Tucker's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Talen Horton-Tucker so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Kenneth Faried

*Javier Gancedo*

Man, I love Kenneth Faried and his overall energy. His first field goal was a one-handed dunk against Paris Basketball and one week later, he was dunking over Walter Tavares! The 36-year-old Manimal is in a great physical condition and that allows him to play above the rim. A. He has been finishing alley-oop dunks since he arrived in Athens, and his connection with Kostas Sloukas is fun to watch. Faried always makes sure he hangs on the rim longer to get Green Nation going. My favorite Faried dunk this season came at home against Baskonia. He took a corner three-point shot that hit the side of the backboard, but got the offensive rebound to fix his mistake with a huge dunk. How cool is that?

content.title: Kenneth Faried's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Kenneth Faried so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

heroMedia.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season

content.text:
*EuroLeague & Friends Global* returns for its second episode, once again hosted by four‑time EuroLeague champion Kyle Hines. This time, the show dives into *"Crown Season"*, a new docu-series created by Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard [Shane Larkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/shane-larkin/007200/). Kyle is joined by Larkin along with two of the series’ guest stars: [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/), AS Monaco’s playmaker, and [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/), Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’ center.

The conversation explores the insights and untold stories highlighted in the series — from their personal investments to Mathias’s road to recovery, plus a behind‑the‑scenes look at their lives away from basketball. Mathias shares his feeling on how he will feel when he finally plays a EuroLeague home game after a long recovery process The players also reveal a lighter side that fans rarely get to see. Kyle asks the trio how the idea for *Crown Season* came about and how Shane managed to bring it to life.

Mike points out that the EuroLeague has huge growth potential, especially if fans begin following not just their teams, but also the players they admire — regardless of which jersey they wear. The group discusses how the perception of players off the court has evolved, particularly after games, and they share a few funny stories along the way. From cultural differences to adapting to new environments, the conversation is relatable and engaging for any viewer.

Toward the end of the episode, Mike, Kyle, Mathias, and Shane debate the idea of a one‑on‑one contest — who should be invited, who would be the favorites, and what rules the competition should follow. Some of their proposed names are obvious, while others are surprising picks you might not expect. And the question remains: would Mike James volunteer himself… or nominate a Monaco teammate?

It’s rare to see four EuroLeague-winning stars openly discussing the league, the culture, and the players from their unique perspectives — so don’t miss *EuroLeague & Friends Global*!

articleMetadata.title: EuroLeague & Friends Global goes deep into Crown Season
articleMetadata.description: EuroLeague & Friends Global returns for its second episode, once again hosted by four‑time EuroLeague champion Kyle Hines. This time, the show dives into "Crown Season", a new docu-series created by Anadolu Efes Istanbul guard Shane Larkin. Kyle is joined by Larkin along with two of the series’ guest stars: Mike James, AS Monaco’s playmaker, and Mathias Lessort, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens’ center.
